# AGENTS.md — test-memory
<!-- Generated by memory-core on 2026-05-11 — auto-sync keeps this fresh; manual refresh: memory-core sync -->

**Type:** backend | **Language:** Typescript
**Backend:** NestJS


## Backend Rules — NestJS
- Feature modules are self-contained — each has its own module, controller, service, DTOs, and entities
- Controllers are thin — validate HTTP input, delegate to service, return response. Zero business logic.
- Services own all business logic — never import controllers or HTTP-specific classes
- Use class-validator and class-transformer with ValidationPipe for all incoming DTOs
- Repository pattern — services depend on repository interfaces, never directly on TypeORM/Prisma
- Guards handle all authentication and authorization — never check tokens inside controllers or services
- Use exception filters for all error handling — never return raw errors or catch exceptions in controllers
- [object Object]
- ConfigModule with Joi/zod validation for all environment variables at startup
- Each module uses forwardRef() only as a last resort — restructure to remove circular dependencies
- Global pipes, guards, and filters are registered in main.ts — never duplicated per-controller
- Use custom decorators to extract common parameter logic from controllers

### Structure
- src/modules/{feature}/
- src/modules/{feature}/{feature}.module.ts
- src/modules/{feature}/{feature}.controller.ts
- src/modules/{feature}/{feature}.service.ts
- src/modules/{feature}/dto/
- src/modules/{feature}/entities/
- src/modules/{feature}/repositories/
- src/common/guards/
- src/common/filters/
- src/common/interceptors/
- src/common/pipes/
- src/common/decorators/
- src/config/

### Never
- Business logic in controllers
- Direct TypeORM/Prisma calls in services (use repositories)
- Circular module dependencies
- Hardcoded configuration values
- Using @Res() or @Next() in controllers — breaks interceptors and exception filters
- Global state outside the DI container
- Catching exceptions inside controllers instead of using filters
- Fat modules that own too many features

---
## Relevant Memory from Previous Projects
- [rule · nestjs] Every business rule, calculation, and orchestration lives in a service. Services are @Injectable() and framework-agnostic — they never import @nestjs/common HTTP decorators or Express/Fastify types.
- [rule · nestjs] Use @nestjs/event-emitter to decouple side effects (send email, invalidate cache, log analytics) from the main business operation. Emit events from services, handle them in dedicated listener classes.
- [rule · nestjs] Use ConfigModule.forRoot() with a Joi or zod validation schema. The app must fail to start if required env vars are missing or have the wrong type. Never access process.env directly anywhere in the app.
- [pattern · nestjs] Dynamic modules (database, cache) use forRoot() for static config and forRootAsync() when config depends on other providers like ConfigService.
- [rule] Version all public APIs from day one via URL prefix (/v1/) or header. Never make breaking changes to existing versions.
- [rule] Review and update dependencies at least monthly. Use automated tools (Dependabot, Renovate) to get PRs for new versions. Never ignore security advisories.
- [rule] Use UUIDs or slugs as public identifiers in API URLs and responses. Never expose sequential database integer IDs to clients.
- [rule] Sanitize all user-generated content before rendering. Escape HTML, strip scripts, and use CSP headers to prevent XSS.
- [rule · nestjs] Group related config using registerAs(&quot;database&quot;, () &#x3D;&gt; ({ url: process.env.DATABASE_URL })). Inject with @InjectConfig(&quot;database&quot;) for typed access. Never use magic strings to access config keys.
- [rule · nestjs] Services throw NotFoundException, ForbiddenException, BadRequestException, ConflictException — not raw Error objects. The global exception filter catches and formats them with the correct HTTP status automatically.
- [rule · nestjs] Never use synchronize: true in any environment other than a throw-away local database. All schema changes are made through migrations generated via TypeORM CLI and committed to source control.
- [rule] Use soft deletes (deleted_at timestamp) for user-generated data. Hard delete only for PII removal on request.
- [rule] Attach a unique correlation/trace ID to every request. Propagate it through all downstream calls. Include it in logs and error responses.
- [rule] Use DTOs for all API responses. Never expose raw database models to the client.
- [pattern · nestjs] Extend CreateUserDto with PartialType(CreateUserDto) to create UpdateUserDto. All fields become optional automatically with no duplication of validators.
- [rule · nestjs] Controller methods never return TypeORM/Prisma entities. Always map to a response DTO before returning. Use plainToInstance(ResponseDto, entity) or a dedicated mapper class.
